articles.title: A tech worker in China is laid off and replaced by AI. Is it legal?
articles.summary: A tech worker in eastern China's Hangzhou city was dismissed after his job was replaced by AI. An appeals court in the city has ruled the dismissal unlawful.

articles.title: The U.S. once had a monopoly on rare earths. How it lost to China
articles.summary: Planet Money tells the story of how the U.S. lost to China on rare earths.

articles.title: Republicans Want Tennessee’s Last Democratic House District
articles.summary: Republicans sliced Nashville into three G.O.P.-leaning congressional districts in 2022. After the Supreme Court decision on voting rights, Memphis could be next.

articles.title: As Israel Entrenches, Frustration With Hezbollah Turns to Support
articles.summary: With the cease-fire fraying and Israel demolishing villages in the south, many Hezbollah supporters in Lebanon are putting aside their annoyance with the group and turning to it for protection.

articles.title: What to Know About the Maine Senate Race After Janet Mills Drops Out
articles.summary: The exit of Gov. Janet Mills kicked off the general election early in what is likely to be one of the most important, expensive and combative Senate races of 2026.

articles.title: Record-breaking DHS shutdown ends. And, May Day protests to draw crowds nationwide
articles.summary: Congress has ended the record-breaking shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security. And, May Day demonstrations across the U.S. are expected to draw crowds protesting the Trump administration.

articles.title: How well can EVs handle the heat — and the cold? AAA put them to the test
articles.summary: Electric vehicles lose some range in the winter — and, to a lesser degree, in the summer. But exactly how much? AAA has brand-new data.

articles.title: Billionaire Chris Larsen Plans to Spend $3.5 Million in NY House Race Amid Midterm Clash Over A.I.
articles.summary: Chris Larsen, who hails from California, plans to spend $3.5 million to help Alex Bores, a New York congressional candidate at the center of a proxy war over A.I. regulation.

articles.title: Obamacare Enrollment Drops Sharply as Costs Rise
articles.summary: Americans can’t afford the higher health insurance premiums that resulted from Congress’s refusal to extend federal tax credits.

articles.title: Fill in the blank for the quiz: The Trump admin took aim at _____ this week
articles.summary: This week, the federal government's been busy. There are paint jobs, fresh indictments, commemorative items and more. If you've been paying attention — good job!

articles.title: How to talk to boys about looksmaxxing
articles.summary: The trend among boys and young men of optimizing their physical appearance includes dangerous practices. Experts offer advice on how to talk to their sons about body image and healthy behaviors.

articles.title: It's Day 1 of Medicaid work requirements in Nebraska. People are worried
articles.summary: Starting May 1, many people covered by Medicaid in Nebraska have to prove they are working. It's a requirement most states will have to implement under President Trump's budget law, beginning in Janua

articles.title: 'Decimate' means much more today than it did in ancient Rome
articles.summary: Today, most people know the word as a synonym for "destroy." But fewer realize its origins — or that it's come to mean something strikingly different than it once did.

articles.title: Nationwide May Day protests expected to pick up mantle of 'No Kings'
articles.summary: The protest organizers are calling for a boycott of work, school and shopping to protest Trump administration policies and what activists describe as a billionaire takeover of government.

articles.title: Myanmar attempts to rehabilitate image with Suu Kyi move
articles.summary: State television in Myanmar says detained opposition leader Aung San Suu Kyi has been transferred from prison to house arrest, more than five years after the military coup that removed her from power.

articles.title: Man charged with attempted murder after stabbings of Jewish men in London
articles.summary: A 45-year-old man was charged with attempted murder in the stabbings of two Jewish men in London, the latest in a string of attacks that have sparked fear and anger in Britain's Jewish community.

articles.title: Hegseth faces questions about Iran in first congressional appearance since war began
articles.summary: Making his first appearance before Congress since the Trump administration went to war against Iran, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced withering questioning from skeptical Democrats.

articles.title: The Venice Biennale jury resigns amid tensions over awards ban, Russian participation
articles.summary: The international jury of the Venice Biennale resigned Thursday amid tensions over Russia's participation and the panel's decision to bar prizes for countries accused of crimes against humanity.

articles.title: Trump’s Plans to Boost Weapons Production Might Not Deliver for Years
articles.summary: While the defense industry has announced plans to make more munitions, much of that expanded production will not quickly kick in.

articles.title: Hezbollah Using Fiber-Optic Drones Against Israeli Targets
articles.summary: The Lebanese militant group is attacking Israeli troops with explosive drones controlled by fiber-optic cables, like those commonly used in the war in Ukraine.

articles.title: Supreme Court Voting Rights Ruling Could Fuel New Era of Redistricting Wars
articles.summary: The expected flood of new congressional maps is likely to produce fewer competitive districts, fewer ways for voters to hold elected officials accountable and more polarized politics.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
